problemTitle: Length of Longest Subarray With at Most K Frequency

sources.code:
# Time:  O(n)
# Space: o(n)

import collections


# freq table, two pointers, sliding window
class Solution(object):
    def maxSubarrayLength(self, nums, k):
        """
        :type nums: List[int]
        :type k: int
        :rtype: int
        """
        cnt = collections.Counter()
        result = left = 0
        for right in xrange(len(nums)):
            cnt[nums[right]] += 1
            while not (cnt[nums[right]] <= k):
                cnt[nums[left]] -= 1
                left += 1
            result = max(result, right-left+1)
        return result

# Time:  O(n)
# Space: o(n)

import collections


# freq table, two pointers, sliding window
class Solution(object):
    def maxSubarrayLength(self, nums, k):
        """
        :type nums: List[int]
        :type k: int
        :rtype: int
        """
        cnt = collections.Counter()
        result = left = 0
        for right in xrange(len(nums)):
            cnt[nums[right]] += 1
            while not (cnt[nums[right]] <= k):
                cnt[nums[left]] -= 1
                left += 1
            result = max(result, right-left+1)
        return result

import collections


# freq table, two pointers, sliding window
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Only shrink when the current window violates the cap, and only as much as needed to restore legality.
class Solution(object):
    def maxSubarrayLength(self, nums, k):
        """
        :type nums: List[int]
        :type k: int
        :rtype: int
        """
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Frequency bookkeeping, targeted window repair, and trusting the monotonic left-to-right rhythm.
        cnt = collections.Counter()
        result = left = 0
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for right in xrange(len(nums)):
            cnt[nums[right]] += 1
            while not (cnt[nums[right]] <= k):
                cnt[nums[left]] -= 1
                left += 1
            result = max(result, right-left+1)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return result

sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Only shrink when the current window violates the cap, and only as much as needed to restore legality.
class Solution:
    def maxSubarrayLength(self, nums: List[int], k: int) -> int:
        # [Reader note] State setup
        # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Frequency bookkeeping, targeted window repair, and trusting the monotonic left-to-right rhythm.
        cnt = defaultdict(int)
        ans = j = 0
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for i, x in enumerate(nums):
            cnt[x] += 1
            while cnt[x] > k:
                cnt[nums[j]] -= 1
                j += 1
            ans = max(ans, i - j + 1)
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return ans

writeups.title: 2958.Length-of-Longest-Subarray-With-at-Most-K-Frequency
writeups.excerpt: ### 2958.Length-of-Longest-Subarray-With-at-Most-K-Frequency 本题就是维护一个滑窗，使得滑窗内的任何元素的频次不能超过K。如果满足条件，那么就移动右边界；如果不满足，那就移动左边界。
